meta.title: Gastown Working Corridor (deterministic fallback)
meta.source: Offline City of Vancouver Open Data exports
buildNotes: Working fallback corridor is active because required offline civic source files are missing.

This fallback now stages Water Street as a compact exploration district with plaza, storefront edge, alley threshold, transit edge, and landmark corner micro-areas instead of a single bent ribbon corridor.
No refreshed reference inputs were present; deterministic default route staging was used.
meta.provenanceSummary: Offline civic-data build generated from local source exports and normalized for the runtime simulator.

artDirection.label: stylized realism with cinematic Vancouver rain-lighting
pillars: wet cobblestone reflections and low-angle dawn highlights
heritage masonry with richer brick-stone-glass contrast
dense civic clutter and postcard framing around the Steam Clock and Water Street bends

generationNotes.buildings: renderer-compatible derived geometry from footprint centroids/extents/headings (footprints preserved)

landmarks.label: Waterfront Station threshold
landmarks.cue: Station canopies give way to brick-front Gastown blocks and the Water Street bend begins.

landmarks.label: Water/Cordova seam
landmarks.cue: The corridor bends into Water Street and starts reading like a heritage streetscape instead of a starter ribbon.

landmarks.label: Water Street mid block
landmarks.cue: Longer brick storefront rhythm and darker carriageway paving make the street read more like Water Street.

landmarks.label: Gastown Steam Clock
landmarks.cue: A brick-and-stone plaza opens at the intersection and stages the Steam Clock at the corner landmark moment.

landmarks.label: Maple Tree Square edge
landmarks.cue: Beyond the Steam Clock the street broadens and loosens toward Maple Tree Square.

landmarks.label: Cambie rise continuation
landmarks.cue: The eastern leg of the route continues beyond the clock intersection toward the Cambie rise.

exploration.fallbackPrompt: Wander the connected plazas, storefront edges, alley mouths, and landmark corners to build your own short loop.

microAreas.label: Station plaza
stopReasons: Watch people spill out from Waterfront Station.
Reorient before committing to Water Street.
returnReasons: It works as a reliable reset point.
The wider pad gives you a clear view back into Gastown.

microAreas.label: Storefront edge
stopReasons: Use the pocket as a pause between the station and the clock.
returnReasons: It makes a good midway meeting point.
The tighter edge reveals the street from a different angle on the way back.

microAreas.label: Alley threshold
stopReasons: Peek into the service lane without leaving the public edge.
Listen for the quieter pocket off the main block.
returnReasons: It turns the approach into a loop instead of a one-way run.
The threshold frames the Steam Clock corner from the side.

microAreas.label: Cambie / Steam Clock corner
stopReasons: Pause for the clock, seating, and busker activity.
Use the open pad to decide where to head next.
returnReasons: The plaza is the natural social hub.
It links back out to Water Street and Maple Tree Square.

microAreas.label: Landmark corner
stopReasons: Take in the clock and corner facades together.
Catch the diagonal view into Maple Tree Square.
returnReasons: It is the clearest landmark rendezvous point.
The corner keeps changing depending on approach direction.

microAreas.label: Maple Tree Square pocket
stopReasons: Step to the square edge for the crossroads feel.
Use the pocket as the eastern half of the loop.
returnReasons: It rewards a second pass after circling the clock.
The square reads differently when you approach from Cambie.

microAreas.label: Transit edge
stopReasons: Look uphill toward the Cambie continuation.
Feel the district open toward the station/transit seam.
returnReasons: It gives the loop a practical edge instead of a dead end.
The shift in grade changes your sense of the block.

bounds.edgeMessage: Stayed inside the explorable Gastown district.
bounds.resetMessage: Returned to the explorable Gastown district.
